stories.title: Costa’s Putin move triggers doubts ― just as he most needs support
stories.summary: BRUSSELS ― Few of the EU’s current chiefs have dodged criticism as well as António Costa. But is the shine wearing off just as he gears up for re-election? As a crucial few months loom ahead for the European Council president ― he’ll be tasked with herding the bloc’s 27 national leaders toward agreement on the EU’s next seven-year budget ― he has made his first serious misstep, according to diplomats. Often praised for his sure-footedness and ability to build consensus, the former Portuguese pri

stories.title: Czechia: Thousands march in support of public media funding
stories.summary: Prime Minister Andrej Babis' government has been accused of seeking to take control over Czech public media. The plan would leave broadcasters with a 15% funding cut and no guarantees of continued future funding.

stories.title: Secret correspondence claims suggest tensions at top of Iranian government
stories.summary: Former negotiating team member gives shock interview claiming supreme leader’s instructions were not followed Middle East crisis – live updates A former member of Iran’s negotiating team in the previous round of talks with the US in Islamabad is facing the threat of prosecution and dismissal from parliament after he went on the main state broadcaster to reveal what he claimed were confidential letters from the country’s supreme leader. The interview with Mahmoud Nabavian, the deputy chair of Ira

stories.title: From the US-Mexico border to protests in Poland: highlights of PhotoEspaña 2026
stories.summary: Spain’s leading festival of photography showcases the work of more than 300 visual artists in nearly 100 exhibitions across the country PhotoEspaña, Spain’s leading festival of photography, held its official opening in Madrid this month and by September nearly 100 exhibitions will have showcased the work of more than 300 visual artists in the capital and across the country. Loosely corralled under the theme of reimagining, the exhibitions feature work by major figures in Spanish and internationa

stories.title: Romania's Dan taps new PM, breaks democratic norms
stories.summary: President Nicusor Dan has nominated a new prime minister, without consulting Romania’s governing parties. Critics accuse Dan of breaking democratic rules.

stories.title: I challenge the Rothko naysayers to stand in front of his monumental art and not feel awe – Rhiannon Lucy Cosslett
stories.summary: An exhibition in Florence that pairs his giant canvases with Renaissance religious art brought me to the edge of tears. It is the perfect refuge from the infinite scroll As an unbaptised agnostic raised with no religion, the closest I ever really come to a spiritual experience is when I’m standing in front of an artwork. Last week I went to Florence to do exactly that, drawn there not by Michelangelo’s David or Botticelli’s Birth of Venus, but by the works of Mark Rothko, that titan of US abstra

stories.title: „Nem a politikai állításaimat vitatják, hanem a testemet” – lesújtó kutatás készült a nemi alapú politikai erőszakról
stories.summary: Szinte minden magyar képviselőnőnek van a szexizmussal összefüggő története, és átéltek már online, szexualizált vagy pszichológiai erőszakot, de ezt senki nem jelentette közülük. Egy öt európai országot vizsgáló kutatás szerint a rossz magyar eredmények nem függetleníthetőek az Orbán-rendszer működésétől.

stories.title: Budapesten fákat döntött, Debrecenben villámárvizet hozott a vihar
stories.summary: Kidőlt fák, megrongált tetőszerkezetek miatt riasztották a tűzoltókat Budapest több kerületében.

stories.title: Rendkívüli kormányülés lesz hétfőn, Magyar Péter meglepetést ígér a parlamentben
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök azt írta, „ismertetem a bábok eltávolítását és a Vagyonvisszaszerzési Hivatalt + még valamit”.

stories.title: Egyre jobban elmérgesedik a lengyel–ukrán vita, ami már nem várt helyeken is lecsapódik
stories.summary: Zelenszkij egy ukrán katonai egység címével régi történelmi sebeket tépett fel Lengyelországban, mire a lengyel elnök visszavonta a kitüntetését. Az orosz–ukrán háborúról ugyanazt gondolja a két ország, de a helyzet rendezetlenül tartós feszültségekhez vezethet.

stories.title: Az iráni kapus mindent fogott, második csoportmeccsén sem tudott győzni Belgium
stories.summary: A gól nélküli döntetlennek elsősorban az egyiptomiak és az új-zélandiak örülhetnek.

stories.title: Annyi embert rúgnak ki a nagy techcégek a mesterséges intelligencia miatt, hogy Kaliforniában a kormányzónak kellett közbelépni
stories.summary: Idén eddig 153 ezer embert építettek le az óriáscégek, mert feleslegessé váltak az MI miatt, vagy rajtuk spórolnak, hogy több pénz jusson az MI-re.

stories.title: Even in this age of what Mark Carney calls global rupture, do not despair: there is still hope for international law | Nathalie Tocci
stories.summary: Developments in Ukraine and Iran show that the military superpowers are not getting it all their own way Our age of what Mark Carney called global rupture is also often described as following the “ law of the jungle ”, in which the strong do what they can and the weak suffer what they must, with international law shattered and multilateral organisations hollowed out. Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, Israel’s onslaught on Gaza, and the US and Israel’s attacks on Iran and Lebanon seem to confirm this

stories.title: Havi 3500 forint lesz az elektromos közbicikli Budapesten, leteszteltük az e-Bubit
stories.summary: Július második felétől ennyi pénzért el lehet vinni bármilyen biciklit az új Mol Bubi flottából fél órára, sőt, akár kettőt is. A BKK-bérletesek további 20 százalék engedményt kapnak.

stories.title: 14 évesen azt mondták, nincs jövője, 17 évesen egy gyárban dolgozott, most 33 percenként szállítja a német válogatottban a gólokat
stories.summary: A német szövetségi kapitány egyelőre kitart amellett, hogy szupercsereként veti be a vb-n az Elefántcsontpart elleni meccset is eldöntő Deniz Undavot.

stories.title: Magyar Péter a vagyonvédelmi hivatalról szóló javaslat legerősebb változatának támogatására kéri a Tiszát
stories.summary: A miniszterelnök szerint a társadalmi egyeztetés során is szigorodhat a vagyonvisszaszerzési hivatalról szóló tervezet.

stories.title: Vitézy: A Dunakeszi Járműjavító csődje miatt kellett osztrák vasúti kocsikat bérelnie a MÁV-nak
stories.summary: Itthon is lenne elég klimatizált kocsi, csak a közlekedési miniszter szerint mind a járműjavítóban vannak, amit az Orbán-kormány orosz és neres köröknek privatizált.

stories.title: Orbán Anita az osztrák sajtónak: Szijjártóék sok dokumentumot ledaráltak
stories.summary: A magyar külügyminiszter a Der Standardnak adott közös interjút osztrák kollégájával, akit lenyűgöz, milyen tempóban hajtja végre a reformokat az új magyar kormány

stories.title: Csak ketten vettek részt David Hockney temetésén, de pont így akarta
stories.summary: Az ünnepelt brit festőművész végakarata szerint csak élettársa és dédunokaöccse vehetett részt a temetésén.

stories.title: Ha tartósan újra is nyílik a Hormuzi-szoros, kellemetlen meglepetések lassíthatják a hajóforgalom helyreállását
stories.summary: Irán azt állította, megint lezárta a kulcsfontosságú vízi útvonalat. Ezt az USA nem erősítette meg, de már a bizonytalanság is sok hajót visszafordulásra késztetett. Ha a svájci tárgyalásokon a politikai akadályok el is hárulnak, marad még egy megoldandó probléma: az aknák kérdése.

stories.title: Visszautasítja a BYD, hogy környezetkárosítást okozott volna Szegeden
stories.summary: Zajlik a nyomozás a magyarországi beruházáskor elkövetett környezetvédelmi szabálytalanságok gyanújával.

stories.title: A román liberálisok elnököt választottak, majd kizárással fenyegették meg az államfő által kijelölt miniszterelnököt
stories.summary: A belharc a kormányalakítás sorsát is befolyásolhatja.

stories.title: A magyar származású ukrán drónparancsnok szerint a Krím félszigetbe fog belebukni Putyin
stories.summary: Minden diktatúra hirtelen omlik össze – üzente Robert „Magyar” Brovdi.

stories.title: Zelenskyy returns Poland's highest honor as row deepens
stories.summary: Ukraine's president has returned a medal to Poland after a decision to strip him of the country's highest honor. Warsaw has been one of Kyiv's key allies since Russia began its war in Ukraine.

stories.title: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
stories.summary: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

stories.title: Czech government causes alarm with plan to scrap license fee
stories.summary: The Czech government has approved a bill to put the funding of public service media under direct political control. Critics warn that if passed, the bill would put a pillar of Czech democracy at risk.

stories.title: Poland: Arrest after Russian artist and Putin critic killed
stories.summary: Prime Minister Donald Tusk says Lublin police arrested a suspect in the fatal shooting of the Russian national and critical artist known as Semyon Skrepetsky. He said investigators were still seeking a "mastermind."

stories.title: CDC to tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola response in DRC and Uganda
stories.summary: Number of people infected now tops 1,000 though health officials say the global risk remains low Sign up for the Breaking News US newsletter email The Centers for Disease Control and Prevention (CDC) will tap $107m in emergency funding for Ebola outbreak response in the Democratic Republic of the Congo ( DRC ) and Uganda , officials said on Thursday. The continued Ebola outbreak in the DRC comes as Canada, Mexico and the US jointly host the Fifa World Cup , attracting visitors from around the wo

stories.title: Russian dissident artist Semyon Skrepezki murdered in Poland
stories.summary: Skrepezki was known for his scathing caricatures of Russian President Vladimir Putin. His suspected killer has been apprehended.

stories.title: 'Warning fire went up': Couple on board yacht describe encounter with Russian warship
stories.summary: The Russian frigate fired warning shots near a British retired couple on a yacht in the English Channel.

stories.title: Middle East live: US-Iran peace talks underway as strait of Hormuz remains closed
stories.summary: This live blog is now closed. Trump threatens fresh attacks on Iran as peace talks take place in Switzerland There ⁠was and is ‘no restriction’ on ⁠Israeli ⁠soldiers ​to act to eliminate ⁠threats in Lebanon, and that troops would not withdraw from the security zone, ‌Israeli defence minister ‌Israel Katz said in a statement on Sunday, according to Reuters. Israeli strikes killed at ‌least 20 people in Lebanon ​on Saturday, Lebanon’s state news agency NNA reported, ⁠a day after a ​ceasefire with

stories.title: Trump piles on Starmer as UK prime minister appears near the exit
stories.summary: U.S. President Donald Trump said Keir Starmer “will resign” as British prime minister, jumping into the U.K.’s political crisis as Labour MPs brace for a possible change at the top of government as soon as Monday. In a Truth Social post on Sunday , Trump said Starmer had “failed badly” on two issues: immigration and energy, before adding: “I wish him well!” Trump singled out North Sea oil — a frequent dividing line between Trump and European center-left leaders — and wrote that Britain sho

stories.title: ‘Every man for himself’: Europe warned of rising competition for energy from Asia
stories.summary: BRUSSELS — Europe is facing growing competition from Asia for limited natural gas supplies, as national reserves run low and demand soars during the summer. And it’s a contest the EU will struggle to win, analysts and officials warn. Centralized Asian economies like China, Vietnam and South Korea are better equipped to negotiate deals to purchase gas on the spot market, leaving free-market oriented Europe at a disadvantage. The EU gave itself power to coordinate  group energy purchases

stories.title: At least seven people killed in Chicago shootings as Trump renews military call
stories.summary: Mayor says ‘violence has no place in our city’ as president criticizes governor for not accepting national guard troops At least seven people have been killed and dozens injured in several shootings in Chicago since Friday, police said, with Donald Trump once again calling for military intervention in the midwestern city. In a post on Truth Social, Trump questioned why Illinois’s governor, JB Pritzker, had not welcomed military deployment. Continue reading...

stories.title: Ukraine war briefing: Drones strike Russia’s Tyumen oil refinery 2,000km away, says Zelenskyy
stories.summary: Reports from Siberia confirm attack, while Ukrainian president says new weapon has 3,000km range; occupied Crimea under attack. What we know on day 1,579 Volodymyr Zelenskiy has confirmed that Ukrainian drones attacked an oil refinery in Russia’s Tyumen region ⁠in western Siberia, ⁠ more ​than 2,000 km (1,200 miles) from Ukraine . He said Ukrainian company Fire Point had developed new long-range drones capable of ⁠travelling more than 3,000km and they had been “successfully deployed”. In his nig

stories.title: Who will lead WHO after Trump’s retreat?
stories.summary: GENEVA — The race to choose the head of the World Health Organization is a test of who will shape global health after America’s retreat: Europe, rising powers in Asia or increasingly influential Gulf states. Countries will decide next year who should lead the WHO after Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus’ second term ends in August 2027, handing the winner an agency recovering from the loss of its biggest donor, facing calls for institutional reform and the increasing threat of another global health cris

stories.title: Vance says US hopes to ‘transform’ Middle East as Iran talks begin
stories.summary: Vice President JD Vance laid out lofty ambitions ahead of negotiations between the U.S. and Iran to end the war, suggesting that the talks could “transform the Middle East” even as fighting in Lebanon threatens to undermine the perilous U.S.-Iran ceasefire. Vance told reporters in Switzerland on Sunday that President Donald Trump’s hope for the region is that it’s “much different 10 years from now than it was 10 years ago,” while pointing to Iran as the catalyst for volatility in the Middle East

stories.title: Dutch PM apologises for Moluccan soldiers’ mistreatment after Indonesian independence
stories.summary: Rob Jetten acknowledges grief and pain of Moluccan families as crowdfunded monument unveiled in Rotterdam The Dutch prime minister, Rob Jetten, has formally apologised for the “heartless” mistreatment of thousands of Moluccan soldiers who fought for the Dutch colonial army during Indonesia’s struggle for independence. About 12,500 people – men who had served in the Royal Dutch East Indies and their families – came from a group of Indonesian islands to the Netherlands in 1951, many having been gi

stories.title: UK taxpayers want higher levies on big tech companies, survey shows
stories.summary: Two-thirds of respondents support increasing the 2% digital services tax for multinationals Taxpayers want the UK to increase levies on giant global technology companies such as Facebook owner Meta, Google and Amazon, a survey of Britons’ attitudes on corporate taxes suggests. The polling released on Monday by the Fair Tax Foundation – abody providing businesses with certification around responsible tax conduct – found that 67% of respondents believe that the government should charge higher digi

stories.title: Far-right millionaire Abelardo de la Espriella wins Colombia’s presidential runoff
stories.summary: Leftwing opponent alleges vote count irregularities after Trump-endorsed lawyer secures narrow majority The Trump-admiring far-right millionaire lawyer and self-styled “outsider” Abelardo de la Espriella has won Colombia’s presidential runoff, defeating the leftwing senator Iván Cepeda. With 99.98% of ballots counted in the preliminary vote tally, De la Espriella had secured 12.95m votes, or 49.66%, just 249,901 more than Cepeda, who received 12.7m votes, or 48.7%. A further 1.6% of ballots were

stories.title: US-Iran talks strained as Trump threats spark Iranian walkout
stories.summary: Talks expected to continue for rest of the week despite disruption caused by US president’s threat to bomb Iran and kidnap negotiating team High-stakes talks between the US and Iran are expected to continue for the rest of the week in Switzerland, after a tense start that saw Iranian negotiators walk out in protest at a stream of threats issued by Donald Trump on social media. The US president had threatened to bomb Iran and even to kidnap the Iranian negotiating team unless the strait of Hormuz

stories.title: ‘We want a new Albania’: protests against Jared Kushner-backed resort turn anger on government
stories.summary: Opposition to plans for ‘small paradise’ island of Sazan becomes wave of dissent against establishment For Ina Shkurti, like so many Albanians, the island of Sazan has played an outsized role. As a child she bathed in its “always calm and emerald green” waters, as a teenager it figured in her dreams and as an adult it was an indelible part of the memory and desire that drew her back, every summer, to Vlore, her home town across the sea. What Shkurti never imagined was that plans to build a mega-

stories.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency and deploys military to quell anti-government protests
stories.summary: Bulldozers sent in to clear roadblocks that have stifled the country as farmers and Indigenous groups protest against conservative president Bolivia’s president declared a state of emergency on Saturday and deployed soldiers and bulldozers to raze anti-government roadblocks that have paralysed the country. For more than six weeks, unions, Indigenous groups and coca farmers have marched through cities and blocked roads across the country with rubble, logs and debris in protest against the conserv

stories.title: ‘There’s no jobs’: struggle and regret in a Welsh town that backed Brexit
stories.summary: Ten years ago Ebbw Vale had the highest proportion of leave voters in Wales despite huge EU funding, which has not been fully replaced Where Ebbw Vale’s steelworks once stood is now a cluster of gleaming modern buildings including a hospital, a leisure centre and a college. Over the past decade, these public facilities have been joined by a public-private cybersecurity research centre and two tech firms. A new railway station opened at the site in 2015. Yet, during the Guardian’s visit to the We

stories.title: Four months after the horrific Iran school bombing, fears grow that Trump and Hegseth will bury the truth
stories.summary: A secretive investigation into the attack that killed at least 175 has concluded, reports suggest. Will its findings ever see the light of day? The attack on a girls’ elementary school in the Iranian town of Minab was one of the US military’s deadliest civilian bombings in decades. But nearly four months on, the Pentagon has produced no answers about why the military fired a Tomahawk cruise missile into a school on the first day of the war, killing at least 175 people, mostly children. Some crit

stories.title: Scottish police arrest man over Edinburgh attacks
stories.summary: Five people were injured in the attacks on Friday that were described by Prime Minister Keir Starmer as being motivated by "anti-Muslim hatred."

stories.title: US and Iran publicly trade threats as talks take place in Switzerland
stories.summary: US President Donald Trump on Sunday threatened to strike Iran if it did not stop Hezbollah from "causing trouble", while Iran's chief negotiator Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf warned the US to "be careful with their statements".

stories.title: Russian strikes on eastern Ukraine kill three and injure 22
stories.summary: Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelenskyy said on Saturday that his forces had targeted an oil-processing facility in Russia's Tyumen region.

stories.title: Australia is publishing books too quickly – and everyone is losing out
stories.summary: Thanks to rushed deadlines, financial pressure and overworked staff, titles are going to market before they’re ready – and then sliding from view immediately A Sydney author – I’ll call her Rebecca – vowed never to write another book after the deranging experience of publishing her first. She’s using a pseudonym because one day she might change her mind; the notoriously small Australian publishing industry does not tend to look with favour on authors who complain. When Rebecca was proofing her d

stories.title: Coal companies to reap billions more in taxpayer diesel subsidies as Labor approves new mining
stories.summary: Albanese government under pressure to wind back fuel tax credit scheme for multinational miners as analysis shows cost to budget Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Coal companies could receive an extra $6.2bn in taxpayer refunds for the diesel they use if the Albanese government greenlights just half the mine developments up for approval. The finding, in an analysis released by activist group Lock the Gate, comes as the government faces an internal campaign before next

stories.title: Taxi and Uber rider targeted in suspected anti-Muslim attacks in Edinburgh, say witnesses
stories.summary: White Scottish man, 38, charged after five men were injured in spate of attacks in city on Friday night Witnesses to the alleged knife attacks on Muslims and others in Edinburgh on Friday have described seeing a taxi and an Uber bike courier being targeted in Leith. The attacks, suspected of being directed against Muslims and people of colour, began near a mosque in the west of Edinburgh, followed by incidents on Leith Walk in the east of the city. Continue reading...

stories.title: Utah wildfire forces evacuation of small town as extreme heat roasts US west
stories.summary: Town of Eureka evacuated due to risk from Iron fire as officials forecast more hot weather in week ahead Extreme heat and dry, windy conditions are fueling multiple wildfires across the US west, including a massive blaze in Utah that forced the evacuation of a small town, with hot weather in the forecast raising the risk of more blazes in the week ahead. The Iron fire in Utah’s Juab county was first detected on Saturday and has so far blackened more than 2,000 acres. The fire, about 70 miles (11

stories.title: Wowcher apologises for email referencing toddler crocodile attack
stories.summary: Company ‘extremely sorry’ for ‘unacceptable’ email urging customers to ‘Snap up these deals quicker than a croc can catch a kid!’ The discount voucher website Wowcher has apologised after appearing to make reference to a crocodile attack on a toddler at a zoo in an email promoting its offers. A spokesperson for Wowcher said it was urgently reviewing its marketing content after the subject line of an email on Saturday urged customers to “Snap up these deals quicker than a croc can catch a kid!” C

stories.title: Trump says repair work to begin ‘immediately’ on beleaguered reflecting pool
stories.summary: Algae blooms and peeling paint mar $14.2m renovation as president claims pool has been ‘seriously vandalized’ Repair work will begin “immediately” at the troubled Lincoln Memorial reflecting pool in Washington DC, Donald Trump said on Sunday, after suggesting the pool would need to be drained and blaming alleged “vandals” for the disruption. The reflecting pool has been plagued by algae blooms and peeling paint following the controversial recent renovation efforts for America’s 250th anniversary

stories.title: Trump faces fresh bipartisan criticism on Iran deal as Vance hails peace talks
stories.summary: Objections comes as Trump threatens to renew attacks on Iran if it doesn’t rein in its proxy in Lebanon US political figures from left and right voiced fresh objections on Sunday to Donald Trump’s provisional deal with Iran – even as the US president made new threats while Vice-President JD Vance hailed progress during the first round of direct peace talks in Switzerland. Negotiations in Lucerne between the US and Iran have already run into difficulties, after Trump wrote on Truth Social that “I

stories.title: Almost three tonnes of cocaine found buried under Sydney property in Australia’s biggest ever seizure, police say
stories.summary: Australian federal police arrested and charged two men after allegedly finding 2.7 tonnes of cocaine in ‘bunkers’ under shipping containers Follow our Australia news live blog for latest updates Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Police claim to have made Australia’s biggest ever cocaine bust after finding $800m worth of the drug buried under false flooring on a semi-rural property. Two men, aged 21 and 25, allegedly tried to run from police and were arrested on Friday

stories.title: Planes were just 300ft apart in Boston airport near miss, expert says
stories.summary: FAA investigating incident involving Delta and American jets that forced Delta plane to abort landing attempt A Delta jet was roughly 300ft (90 meters) from an American Airlines plane during a close call at Boston’s airport that forced the Delta aircraft to abort a weekend landing attempt, an aviation expert said on Sunday. The Federal Aviation Administration said it was investigating the incident between two commercial flights that happened Saturday at Boston Logan international airport. Contin

stories.title: Lloyds Banking Group to hire 300 tech experts to work on AI
stories.summary: Exclusive: While recruits will increase headcount for now, broader adoption of AI could lead to job cuts in future Lloyds Banking Group has launched an AI recruitment drive for 300 tech experts, weeks before its chief executive, Charlie Nunn, announces a strategic plan for the 261-year-old lender. The bank said it intended the recruits to work on its use and development of agentic AI by September, referring to autonomous AI models that can plan and execute tasks with minimal human oversight. Con

stories.title: LA firefighters battle warehouse blaze amid concerns over billowing smoke
stories.summary: Newsom declares state of emergency as crews continue to fight stubborn Boyle Heights fire that has raged for days California’s governor has declared a state of emergency for the city of Los Angeles, as firefighters struggle to contain a stubborn warehouse blaze that has raged for days and blanketed parts of the city in smoke. Gavin Newsom announced he was directing state agencies to provide “additional assistance and resources” to help battle the fire, located in the neighborhood of Boyle Height

stories.title: Australia politics live: Monique Ryan accuses Labor of ‘1,000 days of harm, 1,000 days of inaction’ on gambling reform
stories.summary: Follow today’s news live Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Hume has no problem with more debate on NDIS changes, but criticises ‘horse trading’ between Labor and the Greens Jane Hume is speaking to RN Breakfast now, saying the Coalition has no problem with increased scrutiny over changes to the NDIS should the Greens negotiate a longer inquiry into the scheme the week. I suppose one of our concerns is that perhaps the changes don’t go far enough in addressing the rorts

stories.title: Two-thirds of EU citizens back UK rejoining bloc, survey finds
stories.summary: Poll also finds three quarters of people in Britain want closer ties, with majority accepting free movement Two-thirds of EU citizens would back Britain rejoining the bloc, while most UK voters say Brexit has been bad for the issues they care about and want closer ties, including levels of integration – such as free movement – long seen as toxic, a survey has found. Ten years after the Brexit referendum, the polling by the European Council on Foreign Relations (ECFR) , a thinktank, found 66% of

stories.title: Canada’s policies force asylum seekers into US to face deportation, critics say
stories.summary: Advocates say the Safe Third Country Agreement forces immigrants to head to an unsafe country: the United States It was the threat of gang violence in Honduras that pushed Carlos and Antonia to flee their home. In 2021, with their toddler, Alejandro, and a handful of belongings, the married couple ventured north hoping to reach safety in the US. The journey, through Guatemala and Mexico , was filled with danger and uncertainty . Continue reading...

stories.title: Sweat, tears and camaraderie as 20,000 runners take on world’s largest ultramarathon
stories.summary: For one day every June, South Africa’s searing racial inequality seems to melt away at Comrades race In the early morning dark, thousands of runners waited, jostling with anticipation. South Africa’s national anthem rang out. Then the haunting swell of Shosholoza , first sung by Zimbabwean migrant workers in South Africa’s goldmines. Finally, that unmistakable, spine-tingling piano: Chariots of Fire. Runners gather before the start of the marathon Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘This changes everything’: how Brexit altered Scotland’s political landscape
stories.summary: Former party leaders reflect on the turbulence that followed the referendum in which most Scottish voters backed the losing side The decision to quit the EU bolstered support for Scottish independence, which a decade after the Brexit referendum is at near record levels, according to Scottish Labour’s former leader Kezia Dugdale. Dugdale said the Brexit vote “creates a frame around fairness” for many in Scotland because, unlike England, Scottish voters comprehensively backed remain in 2016, by 62

stories.title: Colombians vote in runoff election expected to trigger shift in decades-long armed conflict
stories.summary: Frontrunner Abelardo de la Espriella has vowed to return to full-scale military confrontation with armed groups Ghost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff vote Colombians are going to the polls in a presidential runoff expected to trigger a dramatic shift in the country’s decades-long armed conflict, now at its most violent point since the landmark 2016 peace agreement between the government and most of the Revolutionary Armed Forces of Colombia (Farc). Polls sh

stories.title: AI scandal rocks the German media
stories.summary: Two leading German newspapers have deleted articles created with the use of artificial intelligence. Many fear an increasing reliance on AI will damage the credibility of German media outlets.

stories.title: Ethiopia's ruling party wins overwhelming majority in parliament
stories.summary: Prime Minister Abiy Ahmed’s Prosperity Party comfortably secured another parliamentary majority, defeating a fragmented opposition.

stories.title: Severe heat wave sweeps Europe, triggering alerts and disruptions
stories.summary: Authorities accross Europe have warned of temperatures exceeding 39 and 40 degrees Celsius as a 'heat dome' sets in. Germany also faces the threat of extreme thunderstorms.

stories.title: Deniz Undav: Germany's 2026 World Cup supersub
stories.summary: Deniz Undav is the man in focus for Germany, but things could have turned out very differently for the Stuttgart striker.

stories.title: Colombia election: Hard-right candidate claims victory
stories.summary: Right-wing presidential candidate, Abelardo de la Espriella, won 49.66% of the Colombians' votes, according to preliminary results. Outgoing President Gustavo Petro said "neither candidate can be declared president."

stories.title: US-Iran talks end in Switzerland, mediators say
stories.summary: According to mediators from Qatar and Pakistan, the talks between United States and Iranian officials in Switzerland have ended with both sides agreeing on a roadmap. Technical talks will continue.

stories.title: Ukraine hits Russia-controlled Crimea in deadly drone attack
stories.summary: Footage of fires at a major depot on Crimea circulated on social media. The Moscow-aligned governor of Crimea has halted public fuel sales after the attack.

stories.title: UK's Starmer mulls 'political realities' after Burnham by-election
stories.summary: A member of UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's Cabinet said it would be "delusional" to pretend there was no threat to his position. But he also said he had no information suggesting he might quit, as a newspaper reported.

stories.title: Ethiopia PM's party wins landslide as fears grow of new conflict
stories.summary: Abiy Ahmed's party retains its huge majority despite unrest in several parts of Ethiopia and tensions with its neighbours.

stories.title: Parisians cool off in canal amid 'red alert' heatwave
stories.summary: France has issued red heatwave alerts for around half the country as temperatures soar to record levels.

stories.title: First round of US-Iran talks end with 'encouraging progress', mediators say
stories.summary: The US-Iran talks began on Sunday in Switzerland, after last week's agreement, which includes a commitment to reach a final deal within 60 days.

stories.title: US-Iran peace deal talks underway in Switzerland

stories.title: Fuel sales halted in occupied Crimea as Ukraine targets oil facilities
stories.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

stories.summary: This blog has now closed Get our breaking news email , free app or daily news podcast Ted O’Brien distanced himself from Pauline Hanson ’s suggestion that Australia shouldn’t give aid to Pacific countries that also take aid from China. He said it was a legitimate concern, but her solution was “completely wrong” for the Pacific and not in Australia’s national interest. The idea that you effectively hold a gun to the head of our Pacific neighbours – that’s not what a friend does, that’s not a way

stories.title: An Armenian tycoon has a private zoo. Now he wants the world’s biggest Jesus statue
stories.summary: Gagik Tsarukyan hopes project will resonate with global movement that blends religious faith, nationalism and cultural conservatism Behind the walls of a sprawling estate on the outskirts of Yerevan, six tigers prowl behind a fence, three lions pace their enclosures, and alligators bask in the afternoon heat. Further into the compound, more animals appear. Beneath a gilded, hand-painted ceiling, a dining hall houses a taxidermy menagerie: white tigers reared on their hind legs, a stuffed eagle p

stories.title: A Ukrainian family built a community in Cleveland. Now, they face deportation
stories.summary: After the US said seeking affordable medical care for their son would not impede their re-entry, Tamila Vashchuk and her 10-year-old were issued removal orders Tamila Vashchuk and her husband, Mykola, are minor celebrities in this corner of Ohio. The Ukrainian couple have appeared on the cover of local magazines and been invited onto morning television shows. En route to building a successful pierogi food business , they’ve met with the governor. A recent law graduate from Cleveland State Univer

stories.title: Sudan: UN warns of mass atrocities in El-Obeid
stories.summary: The United Nations Security Council has called on the paramilitary RSF in Sudan to not launch an attack on the city of El-Obeid.

stories.title: Germany could scrap law banning insults against politicians
stories.summary: Germany's special law prohibiting the insulting of politicians has led to cases that many people consider absurd. Some members of the government want to abolish the law; others think there's a good reason to retain it.

stories.title: Stonehenge revelers ring in summer solstice
stories.summary: Thousands celebrated through the night at Stonehenge in southwest England, ushering in the sunrise on the longest day of the year in the Northern Hemisphere. The stone circle aligns with the sun's path at the solstices.

stories.title: How TikTok-style videos keep human brains hooked on content
stories.summary: TikTok, Instagram and YouTube clips are designed to overwhelm the brain's pleasure circuitry and keep people watching. Researchers say short-form videos may only be the start when it comes to harvesting human attention.

stories.title: Former Olympian denies vandalising Washington Reflecting Pool after arrest
stories.summary: Davey Hearn says he was simply touching the new paint at the site out of curiosity and did not remove or alter it.

stories.title: Dutch royals enjoy two big results in one World Cup day
stories.summary: There may be about 5,000 miles between the Netherlands and their former colony Curacao but members of the Dutch royal family managed to see both sides play on Saturday.

stories.title: ‘A flying White House’: President Trump unveils the new Air Force One
stories.summary: Converted from a former Qatari-owned Boeing 747, the new Air Force One will serve as a temporary presidential aircraft until Boeing delivers the long-delayed replacement fleet in 2028.

stories.title: Tight security as Indian students resit medical exam after alleged paper leak
stories.summary: Millions of candidates face biometric checks and frisking as air force deployed to secure exam papers.

stories.title: Indian cricketer, 15, hits record-breaking 50 in just 11 balls
stories.summary: Vaibhav Sooryavanshi breaks the record for the fastest 50-over List A half-century in history on the same day he was not included in India's one-day international squad.

stories.title: Low-key funeral held for giant of the art world David Hockney
stories.summary: The artist, who passed away at his home in London earlier this month, requested just his partner and great-nephew attend.

stories.title: A kánikulával együtt a vízhiányra való figyelmeztetés is megérkezett
stories.summary: Ráckeve, Szigetbecse és Dömsöd környékén nyomáscsökkenés lehet a megnövekedett vízigény miatt.

stories.title: A BL-ben és a 2028-as foci-Eb-n nem lesznek ivószünetek
stories.summary: Az UEFA nem tervez szabálymódosítást, míg a FIFA egyelőre nem ismertette a 2030-as vb-re vonatkozó terveit.

stories.title: Rongálással vádolnak egy olimpikont, pedig a Lincoln medencére Trump miatt felvitt festék amúgy is foszlik
stories.summary: Az egykori kenus szerint nem tépett le semmit a festékrétegből, csak felkapta a víz színén úszó darabot. Az elnök külön posztban szörnyülködött a barbárságon.

stories.summary: Az ügyben feljelentést tesznek, a zászlókat pedig néhány napon belül pótolják. Videó is készült arról, hogy valaki leszedi, és a Dunába dobálja a zászlókat, fényes nappal.

stories.title: Megvan Yamal első vb-gólja, a spanyolok magabiztosan verték Szaúd-Arábiát
stories.summary: A végső győzelemre is esélyes spanyol válogatott szűk fél óra alatt eldöntötte a három pont sorsát.

stories.summary: Társai negyed órán át nem vették észre, hogy barátjuk elmerült.

stories.summary: Hegedűs Zsolt egészségügyi miniszter már a megjelenése előtt reagált a videóra.

stories.title: A szerelmes lányok szomorúbbak, mint gondolnád
stories.summary: Olivia Rodrigo eddigi legérettebb és koherensebb lemeze, a You Seem Pretty Sad for a Girl So in Love bizonyítéka annak, hogy nem szégyen szeretni, majd kudarcot vallani egy kapcsolatban.

stories.title: Vissza se juttok a kib... országotokba! – üzente Trump a Hormuzi-szorosról tárgyaló irániaknak
stories.summary: Amerikai elnök ezzel akart lendületet adni a Vance alelnök vezette amerikai delegációnak az iráni féllel való svájci tárgyaláshoz. Az irániak szerint Trump fenyegetése a kétségbeesés jele.

stories.title: Meghalt a Doom és a Duke Nukem legendás zeneszerzője
stories.summary: Bobby Prince 81 éves volt. Az 1990-es években olyan hangokat csiholt ki a korabeli pécékből, mint senki más.

stories.title: Óriási versenyben találta meg Izland a világ legrosszabb fotósát
stories.summary: Nem könnyű 127 ezerből a legutolsónak lenni, egy 28 éves francia nőnek sikerült az izlandi légitársaság versenyén.

stories.title: Uruguay–Zöld-foki Köztársaság 2-2
Új-Zéland–Egyiptom 1-3
Nem sokkal a letartóztatások előtt szerződött 9 milliárdra a XIII. kerület a parkfenntartási botrány egyik cégével
stories.summary: A szerződést utólag csak akkor lehetne felmondani, ha a cég nem vagy rosszul végezné a munkáját. A kerület több tízmilliárd forintra szerződött a vállalattal az elmúlt években. Ez már a negyedik sokmilliárdos szerződés a felek között.

stories.title: Spain: PM's wife must face corruption trial, judge rules
stories.summary: A court in Madrid ruled that there is sufficient evidence against Begona Gomez, the wife of Prime Minister Pedro Sanchez. The 55-year-old university director is banned from leaving the country.

stories.title: 16 dead and 12 injured following Israeli strikes, Lebanon's civil defence agency says
stories.summary: It comes less than a day after US officials said Israel and the Iran-backed militant group Hezbollah had agreed to a renewed ceasefire.

stories.title: Zelenskyy issues one-week ultimatum to Lukashenka over drone-guidance equipment
stories.summary: During a media briefing in Kyiv on Friday, Zelenskyy threatened Ukraine will take down the drone relay equipment on the border if the Belarusian leader, Aliaksandr Lukashenka, doesn't do anything to address it.

stories.title: Trump threatens Hormuz tolls as ships sail on despite IRGC's closure claim
stories.summary: Iran's IRGC said it had closed the Strait of Hormuz over Israeli strikes on Lebanon, but CENTCOM said 55 ships carrying 17 million barrels of oil transited unhindered. Trump meanwhile warned of US tolls if a final deal was not reached within 60 days.

stories.title: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
stories.summary: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

stories.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

stories.summary: Israel's military said the soldiers were killed "in combat" when their tank was hit during an operation near Kfar Tebnit.

stories.title: Lebanon air raids kill 18 as residents flee southern villages
stories.summary: Lebanon reported 18 deaths on 19 June after Israeli airstrikes hit several locations in the country's south and east, raising fresh concerns about the durability of a regional peace initiative announced earlier this week.

stories.title: Ghost of far-right paramilitaries hovers over Colombia’s presidential runoff vote
stories.summary: Colombians will choose on Sunday between two men whose lives have been very differently shaped by the militias, and whose visions for the country are poles apart Whoever wins Sunday’s presidential runoff vote in Colombia, the country’s next leader will have a personal history intertwined with one of the criminal forces at the heart of a decades-long armed conflict that claimed nearly half a million lives. The lives of Iván Cepeda and Abelardo de la Espriella have, in very different ways, been sh

stories.title: Sok mindent jól csináltak, aztán elúszott a meccs, mondta a svéd kapitány a hollandok elleni 5-1-es vereség után
stories.summary: Graham Potter szerint Hollandia tarthatatlan volt a széleken.

stories.title: Az extrém meleg miatt több helyen megtiltották az alkoholfogyasztást Franciaországban
stories.summary: Épp akkor, amikor egy több millió embert érintő országos fesztivált tartják. Egyes helyeken akár 41 fok is lehet.

stories.title: A holland király is megtapsolta az Ecuador ellen mindent fogó curaçaói kapust
stories.summary: Eloy Room azzal viccelt a történelmi vb-pontszerzés után, hogy akár szobrot is állíthatnának neki odahaza.

stories.title: Soha nem volt még nagyobb szükségünk a cselekvő reményre, mint most
stories.summary: 2016-ra oda jutott az emberiség, hogy a világ népességének 54 százaléka városokban él, miközben évről évre nő a különböző mentális betegségekben szenvedők száma. A háttérben megbújó egyik ok a természettől való teljes elidegenedésünk lehet, a jó hír viszont az, hogy ezt a kapcsolatot akár már heti 3x20 perccel újra kiépíthetjük, ha követjük Mihaletzky Luca Zöld lélekkel című könyvének tanácsait.

stories.title: Halállal végződött két pétanque-csapat vitája Franciaországban
stories.summary: A meleg miatt a tengerparti játékosok az árnyékos városi pályán szerettek volna játszani. Aztán valakit fejbevertek egy pétanque-golyóval.

stories.title: A gíroszok és dönerek egyszerre vadiújak és ősiek – és otthon is elkészíthetők!
stories.summary: Bár a húsok nyárson sütése talán a legősibb ételkészítési módszer, a dönerek és gíroszok utcán, könyékig szószosan fogyasztandó formája modern európai találmány. Két módszert is kipróbáltunk az otthoni előállításukhoz.

stories.title: Felfüggesztették a lakossági tankolást a Krím félszigeten
stories.summary: Ukrajna az elmúlt hónapok egyik legnagyobb dróntámadását hajtotta végre nemrég a térségben.

stories.summary: A bulizók tűzrakóhelyet akartak építeni, a védett műemléknek számító várrom falából feszegettek ki hozzá köveket.

stories.summary: 11 és 15 óra között mindenki kerülje a napozást.

stories.title: 80 millió font is kevés a Newcastle középályásáért
stories.summary: Sandro Tonalit a hírek szerint csak 100 millió font körül adnák el.

stories.summary: Hónapokat töltött amatőr focimeccsek fotózásával a mexikói fővárosban Raquel Cunha, a Reuters fotósa. Focipályák drónfotókon és a pálya széléről.

stories.summary: Ahol sok az árveréseken beragadt ingatlan, tulajdonrész, ott egy-egy településrész további fejlődése nehézzé vagy lehetetlenné válik, és lefelé tartó spirálba kezd a lakókörnyezet.

stories.title: Kipróbáltuk, hogy van-e élet a Google keresőjén és a Chrome-on túl
stories.summary: Egyre többen szakadnának le az amerikai techóriásokról, úgyhogy megnéztük, milyen európai lehetőségek léteznek böngészőből, zenelejátszóból és egy csomó mindennapos alkalmazásból, és ezek mennyire használhatók valójában.

stories.title: Inequality is elementary to Germany's school system
stories.summary: Children's paths through Germany's school system are often determined before their first day of kindergarten, according to the national report on education. An elementary school in Bonn is offering a new model for kids.

stories.title: France bans alcohol during Fete de la Musique amid heat wave
stories.summary: France is taking additional measures as millions of people gear up for Fete de la Musique under punishing temperatures.

stories.title: Are Ukraine drones really exposing gaps in Russia's defense?
stories.summary: Once seen as almost impenetrable, Russian air defenses are now under growing strain from Ukrainian drones. A recent attack on Moscow has reignited debate over Russian vulnerabilities.

stories.title: Terror police probe suspected anti-Muslim attacks in Edinburgh
stories.summary: Counterterrorism police have taken charge after a series of violent attacks in the Scottish capital that left five men injured.

stories.title: Undav heroics help Germany beat Ivory Coast
stories.summary: In Germany's second 2026 World Cup game, Deniz Undav helped Germany to a comeback win against Ivory Coast in Toronto.

stories.summary: Australia was previously the only continent where the H5N1 bird flu strain had not yet been found.

stories.summary: Travelling with a humanitarian convoy, BBC's Hugo Bachega has been given rare access to a part of Lebanon under Israeli occupation.

stories.title: Why an AI company cleaned my New York City apartment for free

stories.summary: Victims' families say there are ways to be an ethical true-crime fan - not an exploitative one.

stories.summary: Damon Albarn, De La Soul and Moonchild Sannelly talk backstage as Gorillaz play their biggest show.

stories.title: Thousands killed in US-Israeli war on Iran - but experts say true total may never be known
stories.summary: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

stories.title: Israel and Hezbollah agreed to renew ceasefire, officials say

stories.title: Weather tracker: Severe thunderstorms sweep Europe and east Asia
stories.summary: Strong winds and heavy rain batter Slovenia, while France experiences atypical heatwave Severe thunderstorms swept across the Balkans last week, bringing widespread destruction to parts of the region. The storms developed as unstable hot air lingered over the Adriatic Sea while a cold front plunged south-eastward. The front began its journey on 10 June in Slovenia, where the Slovenian Environment Agency recorded 65mph gusts at Ljubljana airport. Heavy rain also fell widely across the region with

stories.title: 'Neither I nor Italy ever beg': Giorgia Meloni rejects Trump's claim she begged for a photo – video
stories.summary: The Italian prime minister posted a video on X saying that Donald Trump's claim she begged him for a photo was 'completely made up'. The US president made the comment to Italy’s La7 TV channel after the G7 summit in France, saying: 'She wanted a picture with me so badly. I wouldn’t have taken it, but I felt sorry for her' Italy PM Meloni ‘stunned’ by Trump’s claims she begged him for a photo Continue reading...

stories.title: Taliban order ban on smartphones as officials shown destroying devices
stories.summary: Directive aimed at government workers, but reports of wider implementation spark warnings of future Afghanistan-wide prohibition The Taliban have ordered a sweeping ban on the use of smartphones by government officials – in what some analysts say could foreshadow broader, population-level restrictions. In a directive issued by the Taliban’s military courts and reviewed by the Guardian, the ban was to take effect this week and prohibits “high rank, low rank, general mujahideen, or service staff”

stories.title: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad built on African roots
stories.summary: Germany's 2026 World Cup squad has leaders with African heritage. The diversity of the team is a strength.

stories.title: US curbs Anthropic AI access, raising global concerns
stories.summary: The Trump administration has restricted foreign access to Anthropic's latest AI models over security fears, forcing a global shutdown. Could the row hurt the Claude-maker's IPO plans and set a far-reaching precedent?

stories.title: Germany's Left Party is dreaming big

stories.title: New exhibitions for Anish Kapoor: Art on the edge

stories.title: Women tackling coal dust and other eco wins this week
stories.summary: Indian women fight back against toxic coal dust, the US hits a solar milestone, and there's good news for soil in Kenya and cars worldwide.

stories.title: Can Europe build its own AI heavyweights to rival the US?
stories.summary: With AI dominated by US firms, Europe is pushing for more independence. New partnerships, investments and startups aim to boost the continent's digital sovereignty, but challenges around funding and regulation remain.

stories.title: As heat waves grow more frequent and fierce, cities worldwide are racing to cool streets and reshape urban life. But can they adapt quickly enough?

stories.title: What to expect from the strongest El Nino in over a century
stories.summary: Warnings of a mighty El Nino weather pattern this year could bring drought, flooding and extreme heat. Can we prepare for the worst of it?

stories.title: German journalist detained in Syria back home, lawyer says
stories.summary: Eva Maria Michelmann vanished in Syria in January as government forces advanced on Kurdish fighters in the northeast. The German government last week said it was working to get her home.

stories.title: EU ban on BPA chemical in food packaging takes effect
stories.summary: From July 2026 onwards, BPA will no longer be permitted in EU food packaging. The industrial chemical is known to interfere with the human hormonal system.

stories.title: Hegseth announces review of US forces in Europe as he lambasts NATO allies in Brussels meeting
stories.summary: Hegseth slammed Europe for prioritising what he called liberal ideals over practical defence needs as he lambasted the alliance’s defence chiefs in a rare appearance at the NATO HQ in Brussels.

stories.title: Do it at home too, women tell Japanese fans who cleaned World Cup stadium
stories.summary: Some see a double standard: Japanese men who clean in public while their wives do all the housework.

stories.title: JD Vance cancels trip to Switzerland for planned US-Iran talks

stories.title: Celebrations as Mexico becomes first nation to reach World Cup knockout stage
stories.summary: Thousands of jubilant Mexico supporters celebrated across the country on 18 June after their team became the first nation to secure a place in the World Cup knockout stage with a 1-0 victory over South Korea.

stories.title: Zimbabwe MPs pass bill to extend president's time in power
stories.summary: The proposal would extend Emmerson Mnangagwa's term by two years and scrap direct presidential elections.

stories.title: Boy, 12, wins hearts after trying to check sick chicken into Ethiopian hospital
stories.summary: Markos's beloved hen was not responding to any treatments - so he did the only thing he could think of.

stories.title: Morocco captain Achraf Hakimi to stand trial for rape

stories.title: Tehran says Strait of Hormuz remains open after confusion
stories.summary: The Persian Gulf Strait Authority has announced that ships that submit "compliant transit requests" would be allowed to transit the Strait.

stories.title: New Russian strikes hit Kharkiv and Odesa, killing one and injuring children
stories.summary: New Russian strikes hit Ukraine's Kharkiv and Odesa regions on 19 June, killing one person, injuring children and adults, and damaging homes, vehicles and fuel facilities overnight.

stories.title: Six-year-old Ebola patient taken from DR Congo hospital found and 'doing well'
stories.summary: Health facilities have come under attack during the current outbreak as a result of misinformation and fear.

stories.title: As Europe bakes, Berlin Zoo’s elephants and bears cool off
stories.summary: Berlin Zoo uses “ice bombs” and water showers to help bears and elephants cool down as Europe faces an intense heatwave this weekend.

stories.title: Heatwave nears 40C in France, forcing tourists to change routines
stories.summary: France is enduring a widespread heatwave, with temperatures nearing 40C in parts of the country and forcing tourists and residents to change their routines.

stories.title: Pétanque player, 68, dies after being 'hit in head with metal boule'
stories.summary: The man was allegedly struck by his 81-year-old opponent, who has since been arrested, the public prosecutor says.

stories.title: Would you choose to take a 22-hour non-stop flight?
stories.summary: The BBC asked Sydney locals if they would take the newly announced, longest ever commercial flight from Sydney to London.

stories.title: US to stop funding HIV programmes in South Africa
stories.summary: More than eight million South Africans are living with HIV – the highest number of any country in the world.

stories.title: Japanese pop group XG went from brutal five year training to global pop stars
stories.summary: The Japanese pop group have become global stars, after being recruited before they were teenagers.

stories.title: James Burrows, legendary director of Cheers and Friends, dies aged 85
stories.summary: Best-known as co-creator of sitcom Cheers, Burrows directed more than 1,000 episodes of other classics including the Big Bang Theory and Will and Grace.

stories.title: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
stories.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

stories.title: Indian outrage over US killing of sailors mounts as leaders attend G7 summit
stories.summary: Relations at lowest ebb in years after Washington refuses to apologise for deaths in strait of Hormuz Fury has continued to mount in India over the US’s refusal to apologise for the deaths of Indian sailors killed in strikes in the strait of Hormuz, further straining relations between the two countries as their leaders meet at the G7 summit in France this week. Last week, three Indian seafarers, who were working on board commercial oil tankers, were killed when the US launched missile strikes on

stories.title: Rightwing MEPs chant 'send them back' after controversial deportation plans are passed – video
stories.summary: Several lawmakers celebrated a vote in the European parliament approving plans to increase deportations of undocumented people. Chants of 'send them back' spurred other MEPs to chant back 'shame on you'. The plans could lead to ICE-style immigration measures being implemented in Europe, which have been criticised by rights groups Continue reading...

stories.title: Is Russia heading for a fuel crisis?
stories.summary: Russia's fuel output is falling after Ukrainian drone strikes on refineries. Seasonal spikes in gasoline and diesel demand could push the system into a deeper crisis, experts say.

stories.title: Moscow uses 'Russian Houses' in Africa to lure recruits into war in Ukraine, investigation shows
stories.summary: Ukrainian military intelligence has revealed that Russia's expanding network of "Russian Houses" functions as a recruitment pipeline for its war, luring young Africans with promises of education and jobs before some are sent to the front lines or into drone factories.

stories.title: Vietnam police rescue hundreds of cats stolen for meat by crime ring
stories.summary: Major operation launched after spate of pet thefts in Ho Chi Minh City, according to local media Police in Vietnam have rescued more than 400 cats in a bust of a cat meat crime ring in Ho Chi Minh City, according to animal welfare groups and local media reports. More than 40 cats were reunited with their owners after the multiday operation last week, but several dozen of those rescued have died due to the harsh conditions in which they were found, the groups said. Continue reading...

stories.title: ‘Period tax’ on sanitary products to be abolished, says Pakistan minister
stories.summary: Campaigners welcome announcement cutting levies on menstrual health items, but say their work to end period poverty is ‘far from over’ Pakistan plans to abolish “period tax”, in a victory for young campaigners who had taken the government to court over the charges. Finance minister Muhammad Aurangzeb announced that sanitary towels and related items were “daily necessities that are indispensable for women’s health, dignity and full participation in social activities”, and said he intended to remo

stories.title: Rejoining customs union would not fix damage caused by Brexit, research finds
stories.summary: Exclusive: Economists find Brexit caused 12% depression in UK exports, most of which is due to leaving single market Brexit has depressed UK exports to the EU by 12%, and rejoining the customs union would undo only a fraction of the damage, research shared with the Guardian shows. With the UK’s future relationship with the bloc likely to feature prominently in a potential Labour leadership contest, the economists John Springford and Anton Spisak, of the Centre for European Reform, provide fresh

stories.title: Barbados prime minister announces manifesto for slavery reparations
stories.summary: Updated document, which emphasises harm done to African women, is being considered by other Caribbean countries Barbados’s prime minister, Mia Mottley, has announced a new manifesto from Caribbean leaders asserting the “moral, ethical and legal case” for reparations over damage caused by hundreds of years of enslavement. Mottley was speaking at a “historic” conference in Ghana to advance the push for reparatory justice after the United Nations adopted a landmark resolution declaring the traffick

stories.title: Bavarian top court rules state spy agency can monitor AfD
stories.summary: Bavaria's regional domestic intelligence agency can monitor the right-wing populist AfD, the state's top court has ruled. Several states are trying to implement similar steps, with the opposition party challenging it.

stories.title: Israel says it's cutting ties to EU's Kaja Kallas
stories.summary: Israel's foreign minister says he is severing contact with the EU's foreign affairs chief Kaja Kallas. Gideon Saar attributed the move to a media report alleging that Kallas compared Israel to apartheid-era South Africa.

stories.title: Drones create the first-ever Fifa scoreboard in Seattle sky
stories.summary: BBC reporter Max Matza is in Seattle, Washington, where 400 drones lit up the night sky on Monday to display the score of the Egypt v Belgium match.

stories.title: Mass protest by ultra-Orthodox Jews in Israel over army conscription
stories.summary: Thousands of ultra-Orthodox Jews protest near Netanya against mandatory army service, demanding release of draft refusers amid Israel’s wartime strains.

stories.title: Video: Hundreds of drones target Russia in major Ukrainian attack
stories.summary: Russian officials said Ukraine launched one of its largest drone attacks of the war. Images and video released by the Russian media showed massive fires raging at the Moscow Oil Refinery, located about 15 kilometers from the Kremlin. Thick black clouds of smoke rose over the city.

stories.title: Kaja Kallas responds after Israeli foreign minister says he will 'sever all contact' with her
stories.summary: Kallas said she valued "dialogue and engagement" with Israel, but she made no mention of the reported apartheid comment.

stories.title: Brussels rally presses EU to curb trade with settlements
stories.summary: Hundreds of demonstrators gathered in Brussels on 17 June ahead of a European Union summit. Protesters call for sanctions on Israel and a ban on goods produced in Israeli settlements.
